blindme.command.effect_already_set: This is the currently active effect
blindme.command.effect_already_unset: There already isn't an effect set specifically for this world
blindme.command.options_locked: The options for this world are locked, and cannot be changed
blindme.command.enforced_by_server: The options for this world are set by the server and cannot be changed
blindme.command.current.none: The is no effect currently active
blindme.options.title: BlindMe Settings
blindme.options.creative_bypass.descript: When enabled, the vision-impairing effect will be disabled when you are in Creative Mode in worlds without specific settings. This option affects all words which do not have this option set.
blindme.options.spectator_bypass.descrip: When enabled, the vision-impairing effect will be disabled when you are in Spectator Mode in worlds without specific settings. This option affects all words which do not have this option set.
The individual settings for the default settings. These settings have no effect if you are in a world which itself has an effect assigned to it.
blindme.options.effect_enabled.descripti: Controls whether or not the vision-impairing effect is active or not.
blindme.options.effect_start.description: This option controls how thick the fog is in relation to the point you can no longer see through it. The lower the number, the closer to the player will the fog obscure blocks.
blindme.options.effect_end.description: This option controls how far away you can see through the fog. The higher the number, the more visibility.
blindme.options.effect_presets.descripti: Instead of manually editing the fog, you can choose to load presets.
blindme.options.world_effect_enabled.des: Controls whether or not to use the globally default effect on this world, entirely disable, or have a custom effect.
blindme.options.current_world_effect.des: The individual settings for this currently loaded world. Changes in these settings do not carry over to other worlds.
blindme.options.world_specific.tooltip: This option only affects the current world.
blindme.options.lock_world.description: If you'd like to undertake a challenge with this world or server, locking the world options will give you an extra step in which to think about if you truly want to back out.
blindme.options.lock_world.screen.title: Are you sure you want to lock the world options?
blindme.options.lock_world.screen.messag: You will be unable to to change any options for this world until you unlock them again.
blindme.options.unlock_world.tooltip: Hold SHIFT to be enable unlocking
blindme.options.unlock_world.screen.titl: Are you sure you want to unlock the world options?
blindme.options.unlock_world.screen.mess: You may fail a self-imposed challenge, or you may get unwanted context or sights on this world!
blindme.options.lock_world.server_enforc: Locking is not supported on server-enforced options
blindme.options.server_enforced.tooltip: This option is currently controlled by the server.
blindme.options.server_enforced.ops_bypa: This server, while in control of world-specific options, allows for operators like yourself to change your personal settings
blindme.server_options.title: Specific BlindMe settings
blindme.server_options.button.tooltip.mu: Change the specific settings for this server. These settings will be instantly applied once you join it.
blindme.server_options.button.tooltip.si: Change the specific settings for this world. These settings will be instantly applied once you open it.
blindme.options.world_effect.tooltip.BLI: Blindness: Blocks and entities are only visible in a small radius
blindme.options.world_effect.tooltip.DAR: Darkness: Blocks and entities are visible in a medium radius
blindme.options.world_effect.tooltip.def: Default: use the effect set as default for all worlds
blindme.effect_description.blindness: The traditional Blindness effect. A very restrictive effect that drastically reduces the player’s range of vision to a radius of four blocks around themselves. All else is rendered in pure black.
blindme.effect_description.darkness: The effect that Darkness has with distant objects. Not as restrictive as Blindness, it reduces the range of vision to a radius of approximately 14 blocks. Does not come with the darkening pulse effect of vanilla Darkness.
blindme.shaders_warning.title: BlindMe Compatibility Waning
blindme.shaders_warning.messsage:
BlindMe has detected that the Iris Shaders mod is currently installed and active. While BlindMe and Iris Shaders do not have compatibility issues themselves, many popular shader packs do not fully consider the Blindness effect or even the fog from the Darkness effect, and will errouneously let you see a larger radius directly or through a transluscent obstacle.

Please be advised that I, the developer of BlindMe myself, recommend AGAINST using shader packs without considerable and thorough testing, as they can fully nullify the challenge of BlindMe.
blindme.error.save.description: Please try saving again. If the error persists, check the logs
modmenu.descriptionTranslation.blindme: A client-sided mod that enforces the Blindness effect on the player, configurable per world.
